# Principles of Secular Critique

**Principle 1: Faith Is Not a Virtue. Believing without evidence is not admirable.**

**Principle 2: The Bible Is Not Moral. The Bible contains immoral commands that Christians reject.**

**Principle 3: Morality Is Secular. We can ground ethics in reason and empathy.**

**Case: The Secular Society. Secular democracies are the most peaceful and prosperous.**

**Case: The Religious Violence. Religious beliefs have caused enormous suffering throughout history.**

**Case: The Moderate Cover. Moderate faith gives cover to extremists.**

**Case: The Afterlife. Belief in an afterlife can make people careless about this life.**
**Case: The Prayer. Prayer does not work. Studies show it has no effect on medical outcomes.**
**Case: The Religious Wars. The wars of religion in Europe killed millions. Religious differences still cause conflict.**
**Case: The Secular Progress. The most progressive societies are the most secular. This is not a coincidence.**
**Case: The Empathy. Empathy is the foundation of secular morality. We do not need God to care about suffering.**
**Case: The Reason. Reason is a better guide to ethics than revelation.**
**Case: The Free Will Defense. Free will does not explain natural disasters. An all-powerful God could prevent them.**
**Case: The Secular State. The separation of church and state protects both religion and government.**
